Description: Government Office Region (England). Mapped from former Strategic Health Authorities.
Notes: APMS used SHAs; data mapped to GORs for compatibility with NHS Digital Bulletin 2024-25.

Description: Unweighted survey respondent count for the region.
Notes: APMS 2014 England total n=13,319 screened; n=7,546 full interview.

Description: % of adults with CIS-R score ≥12 (CMD clinical threshold).
Notes: National figure: 15.7% all adults, 12.2% men, 19.1% women (APMS 2014).

Description: 95% Wilson score CI lower bound on CMD_Prev_Pct.
Notes: Wilson interval preferred over normal approx for small samples.

Description: 95% Wilson score CI upper bound on CMD_Prev_Pct.
Notes: Wilson interval preferred over normal approx for small samples.

Description: Difference in CMD prevalence: Women% − Men% for that region.
Notes: National gender gap: 6.9pp. Individual regions vary 6–8pp.

Description: Chi-square or t test statistic value.
Notes: Null for t-test rows; t_stat column used instead.

Description: Two-tailed p-value for the hypothesis test.
Notes: Significance threshold α=0.05 throughout. All 5 tests: p<0.05.

Description: Age group of respondents per NHS APMS standard bands.
Notes: Age bands follow APMS 2014 / NHS standard groupings.

Description: Gender category as recorded in APMS 2014.
Notes: APMS 2014 collected binary gender. Non-binary not separately reported in published tables.

Description: % of respondents with CIS-R score in band 0–5 (minimal/no symptoms).
% of respondents with CIS-R score in band 6–11 (subthreshold symptoms).
Notes: CIS-R 6-11 = subthreshold; below clinical CMD threshold.

Description: % of respondents with CIS-R score in band 12–17 (moderate CMD).
Notes: CIS-R ≥12 = clinical CMD threshold. Likely to benefit from intervention.
CIS-R ≥18 = severe symptoms; almost certainly benefit from treatment.

Description: Estimated mean CIS-R score using band midpoint weighting.
Notes: Approximation using midpoints [2.5, 8.5, 14.5, 22.0]. Not a direct measurement.

Description: Shapiro-Wilk W statistic for normality test.
Notes: W close to 1.0 = more normal. All segments: W<0.85 — not normally distributed.
